# Solution

## (a) Implementation of `Canonicalize`

```dafny
function Canonicalize(x: Id): Id {
  Id(x.kind, x.value, maxInt(x.width, PAD))
}

function maxInt(a: nat, b: nat): nat {
  if a >= b then a else b
}
```

Or more concisely without a helper function:

```dafny
function Canonicalize(x: Id): Id {
  Id(x.kind, x.value, if x.width >= PAD then x.width else PAD)
}
```

## (b) Lemma specification

```dafny
lemma Spec(x: Id)
  requires Wellformed(x)
  ensures var c := Canonicalize(x); 
          c.kind == x.kind &&
          c.value == x.value &&
          c.width >= PAD &&
          c.width >= NumDigits(c.value) &&
          Wellformed(c) &&
          (x.width >= PAD ⟹ c.width == x.width) &&
          (x.width < PAD ⟹ c.width == PAD)
{ }
```

**Key aspects of the specification:**
- **Kind and value are preserved** — canonicalization never changes what entity is referenced
- **Width is at least 4** — the canonical width respects the minimum
- **Wellformedness is maintained** — the width can hold the value after canonicalization
- **Idempotence on canonical ids** — if the input is already at 4+ digits, the width doesn't change
- **Zero-padding to 4** — if the input is below 4 digits, it's padded to exactly 4
